title: W6.2 — StackedGeneralizationEnsemble (XGB on OOF)
pyright_warnings_note: uv run pyright reports 0 errors on src/kairon/models/stacking.py and tests/models/test_stacking.py. The 0-error count is the W6.2 pyright --strict requirement.

pytest_regression.note: W6.2 adds 2 new tests: test_strict_fold_isolation and test_stacking_handles_missing_xgboost. The first pins the anti-leakage contract (fit only on OOF rows from folds<k; k=0 raises with a clear error). The second pins the xgboost-missing fallback to sklearn.linear_model.LogisticRegression and the end-to-end fit+predict path on the sklearn backend produces a finite p_meta in [0, 1].

stacked_ensemble_class.name: StackedGeneralizationEnsemble
stacked_ensemble_class.backend_selection: If config.use_xgboost_if_available=True AND importlib.util.find_spec('xgboost') is not None: xgboost.XGBClassifier with the config's hyperparameters; otherwise: sklearn.linear_model.LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000, random_state=config.random_state). The fallback is seamless and the public API is identical regardless of which backend is active.
stacked_ensemble_class.xgboost_fallback: sklearn.linear_model.LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000, random_state=config.random_state). The max_iter is bumped from the sklearn default (100) so convergence is robust on small-N meta-feature matrices. LogisticRegression is the natural fallback because the meta-on-meta is a linear combination of base probabilities.
stacked_ensemble_class.fit_contract: fit(features: FeatureMatrix, y: np.ndarray, *, sample_weight=None, loss_fn='cross_entropy') -> TrainedModel (Model contract). For the W6.2 anti-leakage contract, the public helper fit_stacked(stacked: StackedOofTable, *, target_fold: int) pre-slices the stacked table to fold_ids < target_fold and forwards to fit. The target_fold=0 case raises ValueError because fold 0 has no preceding OOF rows under the W3.6 protocol.
stacked_ensemble_class.predict_contract: predict_meta(trained: TrainedModel, X_oof: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray returns the class-1 probability as a 1-D float64 array of length n. The 2-D (n, n_base) array is wrapped in a FeatureMatrix internally so the model's contract is uniform with the rest of the model layer. Mirrors the W3.3 MetaLearnerModel predict surface but is exposed as a public method (not just the standard predict) because the W6.2 contract requires fold-k-only predictions and the W3.3 predict does not enforce that contract.

stacked_oof_table_dataclass.name: StackedOofTable
stacked_oof_table_dataclass.from_arrow_classmethod: StackedOofTable.from_arrow(table, *, base_model_names=None) builds a StackedOofTable from a W6.1 stacked pa.Table. The p_oof_<i> columns are sorted by integer suffix and stacked into a 2-D float64 array. Raises ValueError if fold_id, y_true, or any p_oof_<i> column is missing.

resolve_backend_helper.name: resolve_backend
resolve_backend_helper.behavior: Returns 'xgboost' if config.use_xgboost_if_available is True AND importlib.util.find_spec('xgboost') is not None; otherwise returns 'sklearn'. Used by tests and by the W6.3 CAS-dominance script to log which backend produced the meta predictions.

acceptance_criteria_check.evidence: src/kairon/models/stacking.py created with class StackedGeneralizationEnsemble(MLConfig) (inherits from kairon.models.base.Model[MLConfig], so it integrates with the existing trainer, registry, and mlflow layer). The class has fit / predict / _fit_core / _predict_core hooks matching the standard Model contract, plus the public fit_stacked(stacked, *, target_fold) and predict_meta(trained, X_oof) helpers that encode the W6.2 anti-leakage contract. The StackedOofTable dataclass is a typed view over the W6.1 stacked OOF table; the from_arrow classmethod validates the schema and surfaces a clear error if the input is not a W6.1 stacked table.

tests/models/test_stacking.py::test_strict_fold_isolation passes. The test runs a 3-fold fixture (500 rows, train=200, test=100) with two noisy base models (threshold classifiers on feature columns 0 and 1). For k=0 the fit raises ValueError('no OOF rows with fold_id<0') because fold 0 has no preceding OOF rows under the W3.6 protocol. For k=1 and k=2 the training mask is exactly fold_id<k, the fold-k prediction equals a reference re-fit on the same mask (no numerical drift), and p_meta is in [0, 1] and finite for every row. A bug in fit_stacked that used the wrong mask (e.g. fold_id<=k or fold_id!=k) would fail the mask assertion and the bit-identical comparison.

tests/models/test_stacking.py::test_stacking_handles_missing_xgboost passes. The test forces the sklearn path by setting use_xgboost_if_available=False on the MLConfig. (1) resolve_backend returns 'sklearn' (the deterministic, CI-friendly branch). (2) The end-to-end fit+predict path on the sklearn backend produces a finite p_meta in [0, 1] on the stacked OOF table. The LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000) backend converges on the small-N meta-feature matrix and the y_proba invariant holds for every row.
uv run pyright src/kairon/models/stacking.py tests/models/test_stacking.py reports 0 errors, 0 warnings. The 0-error count is the W6.2 pyright --strict requirement.

W6.2 is additive: src/kairon/models/stacking.py is a new module, tests/models/test_stacking.py is a new test file. No existing module is modified. The W3.3 metalabel module (MLConfig, MetaLearnerModel) is reused unchanged; the W3.6 OOF generator and the W6.1 generate_stacked_oof are reused unchanged. Total new tests in this commit: 2 (test_strict_fold_isolation, test_stacking_handles_missing_xgboost). The 2 tests run on the existing pytest infrastructure with no new dependencies (LogisticRegression is part of sklearn which is already a project dep; xgboost is optional and not required for the test to pass).

deviation_from_spec.ml_config_reuse: Spec said 'class StackedGeneralizationEnsemble(MLConfig)' (literal class extending MLConfig). The implementation has 'class StackedGeneralizationEnsemble(Model[MLConfig])' (Model subclass parameterized by MLConfig), which is the standard kairon.models.base.Model pattern used by MetaLearnerModel (W3.3) and the other model backends. MLConfig is reused from kairon.models.metalabel rather than redefined. Justification: redefining MLConfig would have introduced a second config dataclass with the same fields, which is a layering violation (two configs with the same name is confusing). Reusing the W3.3 MLConfig preserves the contract (n_estimators, max_depth, learning_rate, random_state, use_xgboost_if_available, subsample, extras) and lets the W6.2 ensemble participate in the same trainer / registry layer as the W3.3 meta-learner.
deviation_from_spec.fallback_backend_choice: Spec said 'falls back to sklearn LogisticRegression if xgboost not installed' (LogisticRegression is the explicit fallback). Implementation uses sklearn.linear_model.LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000, random_state=config.random_state). The max_iter=1000 is bumped from the sklearn default (100) so convergence is robust on small-N meta-feature matrices. The random_state is forwarded from config.random_state for reproducibility. No other sklearn classifier is in scope (per the spec).
deviation_from_spec.fit_stacked_target_fold_zero: Spec said 'fit ONLY on OOF predictions from folds<k; predictions for fold k use ONLY the fold-k OOF features as input'. The implementation enforces the contract strictly: target_fold=0 raises ValueError because fold 0 has no preceding OOF rows under the W3.6 protocol. The test pins the rejection. The spec's literal contract is satisfied (a fit on fold 0's own OOF rows would leak fold 0's labels), and the rejection is a defensive add that surfaces a clear error rather than silently producing a leaky fit.

notes: W6.2 ships the stacked-generalisation meta-learner. The model is a thin shell around the W6.1 stacked OOF table, with the W6.2 anti-leakage contract enforced by the public fit_stacked(stacked, *, target_fold) helper. The fallback to sklearn.linear_model.LogisticRegression when xgboost is missing is the deterministic, CI-friendly branch and is pinned by test_stacking_handles_missing_xgboost. The W6.3 CAS-dominance gate consumes the fitted meta to compute the CAS comparison vs the TopK primary; the W6 FALLBACK decision (PROCEED vs skip_stacked_meta) is the next critical decision point.
